description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone featuring a rectangular wooden base with a stepped design and a metallic rotary receiver. The base is made of polished wood with a visible grain pattern, and it includes a small yellow knob or switch on the front lower section. The receiver is metallic, with a cylindrical handle and rounded earpiece and mouthpiece, connected to the base by a straight cord. The top of the base has a metallic post that supports the receiver when not in use, acting as a cradle. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones, with a blend of wood and metal materials. The object exudes a classic, antique aesthetic suitable for historical or decorative urban settings.
description: A vintage corded telephone with a wooden base, metallic receiver, and a straight cord, featuring a classic, antique design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the metallic receiver hanging on the left side, the wooden base, and a yellow knob on the lower front.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the metallic post and receiver, with the yellow knob visible on the left side of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back of the base and the receiver resting on the right side.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the metallic receiver and the yellow knob on the right side of the base.
